name: Open3D (3D Data Processing Library)

performance.notes: C++ backend; GPU acceleration for some algorithms; real-time rendering capable

agent_readiness.notes: 3D point cloud, mesh, and RGBD processing. import open3d as o3d. pcd = o3d.io.read_point_cloud('cloud.ply'). pcd.voxel_down_sample(0.05). o3d.visualization.draw_geometries([pcd]). ICP registration: o3d.pipelines.registration.registration_icp(). Surface reconstruction: o3d.geometry.TriangleMesh.create_from_point_cloud_poisson(). TSDF fusion for depth maps. Deep learning: Open3D-ML with PyTorch/TF. Supported formats: PLY, PCD, XYZ, OBJ, STL, off. Used in robotics, autonomous vehicles, 3D scanning. Real-time visualization via WebRTC (Jupyter) or native window.

gotchas: Visualization window requires display (headless needs EGL or off-screen rendering)
Deep learning module (Open3D-ML) requires separate install
GPU algorithms require CUDA build (not default pip install)
Large point clouds (>10M pts) require voxel downsampling first
best_when: LiDAR point cloud processing for autonomous vehicles/robotics
3D scanning and reconstruction from depth cameras
avoid_when: Medical imaging (SimpleITK or VTK more appropriate)
Headless server processing without EGL setup
summary: Open3D is Intel's 3D data processing library for point clouds and meshes. o3d.io.read_point_cloud('file.ply'). Voxel downsampling, normal estimation, ICP registration. Surface reconstruction via Poisson or Ball Pivoting. TSDF fusion for RGBD cameras. Real-time visualization. Open3D-ML for deep learning on 3D data. Supports PLY, PCD, OBJ, STL. Used in LiDAR processing, autonomous vehicles, 3D reconstruction. MIT license.
